query: In what year was the tallest fixed steel structure completed?When ready, output the final answer enclosed in <answer> and </answer> tags. Do not generate any content after the </answer> tag.
query_analysis:
Concise Summary: The query seeks the completion year of the world's tallest fixed steel structure, requiring factual retrieval of specific engineering records.

Required Skills:
Factual knowledge retrieval, verification of historical engineering records, and precise data extraction.

Relevant Tools:
Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool is optimal for retrieving structured historical data on specific structures; Ground_Google_Search_Tool serves as a backup for real-time verification.

Additional Considerations:
Ensure the distinction between 'fixed' and 'movable' structures, and verify if the record refers to height or weight, as definitions vary.

Action Step 2.sub_goal: Search the web to identify the name of the world's tallest fixed steel structure and determine the year it was completed.
Action Step 2.command: execution = tool.execute(query='tallest fixed steel structure completed year')
result:
The tallest fixed steel structure is the Willis Tower, located in Chicago, Illinois, USA, which was completed in 1974.[1](https://www.hbfabco.com/what-material-was-the-eiffel-tower-built-with/), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/92867-tallest-steel-building), [3](https://www.som.com/projects/willis-tower-formerly-sears-tower/)

Standing at 442 meters (1,451 feet), the Willis Tower, originally known as the Sears Tower, is a 108-story office skyscraper predominantly constructed from steel.[1](https://www.hbfabco.com/what-material-was-the-eiffel-tower-built-with/), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/92867-tallest-steel-building), [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Willis_Tower), [5](https://schroedercmi.com/news/4-of-the-most-famous-steel-buildings-around-the-world/) Its design features an innovative "bundled tube structural system" made of nine steel-unit square tubes.[1](https://www.hbfabco.com/what-material-was-the-eiffel-tower-built-with/), [6](https://www.ternium.com/en/media/news/steel-enduring-legacy--15071842624), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/92867-tallest-steel-building), [3](https://www.som.com/projects/willis-tower-formerly-sears-tower/) While other taller structures exist, such as the Burj Khalifa, their primary construction consists of reinforced concrete with steel elements, rather than being primarily steel structures.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Burj_Khalifa), [8](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHRCdKSzHdGVAlldpGTR8tjZinEruVz-WkEN17X9x1hrsTCCKttF_78L0wykIAPE6Lu6UDIBcND55ZwoHtfmrsFlOYPJFmUVo3XxNdBs7lFgEKmTIwiWqOxxaBusO0NiOs_IqD6), [9](https://planeteco.com/what-are-the-materials-used-to-build-burj-khalifa/), [10](https://www.reddit.com/r/UAE/comments/13s3xny/what_makes_burj_khalifa_so_strong_and_stable/), [11](https://www.goconstruct.org/news-and-features/iconic-construction-projects-burj-khalifa) The Eiffel Tower, a prominent historical landmark, is made of wrought iron, not steel.[1](https://www.hbfabco.com/what-material-was-the-eiffel-tower-built-with/), [12](https://www.quora.com/What-type-of-material-is-the-Eiffel-tower-made-out-of-Why-was-this-chosen-over-something-stronger-or-cheaper-to-produce), [13](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Eiffel-Tower-Paris-France), [14](https://hierrosmoraanton.com/en/the-eiffel-tower-did-you-know-its-not-made-of-steel/), [15](https://www.worldatlas.com/articles/what-materials-were-used-to-build-the-eiffel-tower.html), [5](https://schroedercmi.com/news/4-of-the-most-famous-steel-buildings-around-the-world/)

direct_output:
**Process Summary:**
1.  **Initial Search (Wikipedia_RAG):** The first action attempted to retrieve information about the tallest fixed steel structure using a Wikipedia search. However, the tool returned a list of irrelevant pages (e.g., roller coasters, lattice towers, the Empire State Building) and failed to identify the specific record holder or its completion year.
2.  **Secondary Search (Ground Google Search):** Recognizing the lack of specific data from the first step, a web search was conducted with the query "tallest fixed steel structure completed year." This search successfully identified the **Willis Tower** (formerly the Sears Tower) in Chicago as the tallest fixed steel structure. The search results explicitly stated that the building was completed in **1974**. The results also clarified that while other structures like the Burj Khalifa are taller, they are primarily concrete, and the Eiffel Tower is wrought iron, confirming the Willis Tower's status as the record holder for steel structures.

**Answer:**
<answer>1974</answer>
